revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == Related Topics == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == Related Topics == * Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == Related Topics == * revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == Related Topics == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == Related Topics == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == Related Topics == * (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, revision.comment: Text replacement - "== Related Topics ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | Latitude deg = 34| Latitude min = 41| Latitude sec = 54.00 | Longitude deg = 135| Longitude min = 47| Longitude sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Construction period = [[4th century]] | Burial facility = Unknown | Dimensions = Mound length 127m | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | Excavated items = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | Map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | Label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the shrine at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-scale kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's shape is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like shape{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} == See Also == *
