{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | Burial Facility = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | Special notes = | map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''Address''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | Icon = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Designation "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its other_designation as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == Burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Tombs] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}''' [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun
