Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft|12=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft|12=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft|12=draft}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|12=draft|12=draft}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} == See Also == * Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview

Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft|12=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft|12=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft|12=draft}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|12=draft|12=draft}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft|12=draft}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|12=draft|12=draft}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Shōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]] Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E4%BD%90%E7%B4%80%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:aki Takatsuka revision.comment: 26 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:佐紀高塚古墳 up to rev 99995019
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 佐紀高塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景
（右手前に前方部、左奥方向に後円部） |所属 = [[佐紀盾列古墳群]]（西群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]山陵町字御陵前 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 54.00 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 15.05 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]代 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |規模 = 墳丘長127m |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第48代[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]] |出土品 = （伝）[[埴輪]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「高野陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Nara city |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 佐紀高塚
古墳 }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]高野陵 拝所}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|称徳天皇高野陵 拝所の全天球画像}} '''佐紀高塚古墳'''（さきたかつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]山陵町（みささぎちょう）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]（西群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''高野陵'''（たかののみささぎ）」として第48代[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]（第46代孝謙天皇重祚）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|[[佐紀盾列古墳群]] 西群}}{{small|　左上：[[五社神古墳]]（伝[[神功皇后]]陵）
　下中：[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（伝[[成務天皇]]陵）
　下右：[[佐紀陵山古墳]]（伝[[日葉酢媛命]]陵）
　最下：'''佐紀高塚古墳'''（伝[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]陵）
{{国土航空写真}}}}]] [[奈良盆地]]北部、佐紀丘陵南麓において、丘尾を切断して築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。丘陵上では多くの巨大古墳を含む[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]の営造が知られるが、本古墳はその古墳群の中で[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（伝[[成務天皇]]陵）・[[佐紀陵山古墳]]（伝[[日葉酢媛命]]陵）・[[五社神古墳]]（伝[[神功皇后]]陵）等とともに西群を形成する{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。佐紀高塚古墳自体は、佐紀石塚山古墳・佐紀陵山古墳の南に隣接する形で築造される{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[明治]]以降は[[宮内庁]]により[[天皇陵]]に治定されているため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けており、南北を主軸とする西群の他古墳とは直交する{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘からはかつて[[埴輪]]が検出されたというが詳らかでない{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。墳丘周囲には不連続ながら鍵穴形で幅の狭い周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 この佐紀高塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期後半の[[4世紀]]代の築造と推定される{{Sfn|高野陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。佐紀古墳群の古墳としては中型規模で、墳丘主軸も他の主要古墳とは方向を異にすることから、主要古墳に随伴するランクの古墳であったものと推測される{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在では宮内庁により第48代[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]（第46代孝謙天皇重祚）の陵に治定されている{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}。 なお古墳域は、後世に営まれた[[平城京]]では右京北辺二坊に位置する{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|p=19}}。また近年には古墳西方を流れる秋篠川において河川改修時に多数の大型[[円筒埴輪]]が出土しているが、それらを本古墳のものとする説もある{{Sfn|高野陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）、文久の修陵で称徳天皇の陵に治定{{Sfn|奈良市史 考古編|1971|pp=125-126}}。 * [[明治]]期、宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により称徳天皇の陵に治定。 * [[1889年]]（明治22年）、石柵・鉄扉の改造{{Sfn|奈良市史 考古編|1971|pp=125-126}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：127メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：84メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：70メートル == 被葬者 == 佐紀高塚古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第48代'''[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]'''（第46代'''孝謙天皇'''重祚）の陵に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/14}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）14コマ。{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 409。。『[[続日本紀]]』によれば、称徳天皇は[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）8月4日に西宮寝殿で崩御したため、即日に作山陵司等の諸司が任じられ、[[鈴鹿王]]（[[高市皇子]]次男）の旧宅の場所に畿内・近国の役夫6,300人をして山陵が営造され、8月17日に天皇をその[[大和国]][[添下郡]]佐貴郷の「高野山陵」に葬ったという{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|高野陵（平凡社）|1981}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠陵の「高野陵」として記載され、大和国添下郡の所在で、兆域は東西5町・南北3町で守戸5烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}。 称徳天皇の真陵については、『西大寺資財流記帳』の記述を基にすると、この佐紀高塚古墳（[[西大寺 (奈良市)|西大寺]]の東方に所在）ではなく西大寺の寺域西限の地に存在したと推定される『続日本紀 3（東洋文庫524）』 平凡社、1990年、p. 299。{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|p=13}}。しかし後世に陵に関する所伝は失われ、[[鎌倉時代]]の「大和国西大寺往古敷地図」（[[徳治]]2年（[[1307年]]）以前成立）を初見として本古墳に比定する説が生じた{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。[[江戸時代]]には、さらに北方の[[五社神古墳]]を称徳天皇陵にあてた説も生じている{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。その後、幕末の文久の修陵に際し、「大和国西大寺往古敷地図」等を基に本古墳が改めて称徳天皇陵に比定され、これが現在に踏襲されている{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。 なお、本来の称徳天皇陵の位置については明らかでないが、西大寺西方にある奈良自動車学校の南側丘陵付近に推定する説がある{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]山陵町字御陵前 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道 ** [[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[近鉄京都線|京都線]] [[平城駅]]（徒歩約6分） ** 近畿日本鉄道（近鉄）京都線・[[近鉄奈良線|奈良線]]・[[近鉄橿原線|橿原線]] [[大和西大寺駅]]（徒歩約7分） '''周辺''' * 成務天皇 狭城盾列池後陵（[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]） * 日葉酢媛命 狭木之寺間陵（[[佐紀陵山古墳]]） * 神功皇后 狭城盾列池上陵（[[五社神古墳]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1971|chapter=|title=奈良市史 考古編|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|pages=125-126|ref={{Harvid|奈良市史 考古編|1971}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=林陸朗 「孝謙天皇」|ref={{Harvid|孝謙天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=戸原純一 「高野陵」（孝謙天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|高野陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=高野陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|高野陵（平凡社）|1981}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|高野陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本裕行|year=1989|chapter=称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|author=西崎卓哉|year=2001|chapter=佐紀高塚古墳|title=大和前方後円墳集成|publisher=[[学生社]]|isbn=4311303270|ref={{Harvid|佐紀高塚古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=今尾文昭|authorlink=今尾文昭|year=2014|chapter=|title=ヤマト政権の一大勢力 佐紀古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」093）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787713339|ref={{Harvid|今尾文昭|2014}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[孝謙天皇]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/048/index.html 高野陵] - 宮内庁 {{デフォルトソート:さきたかつかこふん}} [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:孝謙天皇]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 佐紀高塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景
（右手前に前方部、左奥方向に後円部） |所属 = [[佐紀盾列古墳群]]（西群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]山陵町字御陵前 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 54.00 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 15.05 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]代 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |規模 = 墳丘長127m |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第48代[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]] |出土品 = （伝）[[埴輪]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「高野陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Nara city |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 佐紀高塚
古墳 }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]高野陵 拝所}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|称徳天皇高野陵 拝所の全天球画像}} '''佐紀高塚古墳'''（さきたかつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]山陵町（みささぎちょう）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]（西群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''高野陵'''（たかののみささぎ）」として第48代[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]（第46代孝謙天皇重祚）の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|[[佐紀盾列古墳群]] 西群}}{{small|　左上：[[五社神古墳]]（伝[[神功皇后]]陵）
　下中：[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（伝[[成務天皇]]陵）
　下右：[[佐紀陵山古墳]]（伝[[日葉酢媛命]]陵）
　最下：'''佐紀高塚古墳'''（伝[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]陵）
{{国土航空写真}}}}]] [[奈良盆地]]北部、佐紀丘陵南麓において、丘尾を切断して築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。丘陵上では多くの巨大古墳を含む[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]の営造が知られるが、本古墳はその古墳群の中で[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（伝[[成務天皇]]陵）・[[佐紀陵山古墳]]（伝[[日葉酢媛命]]陵）・[[五社神古墳]]（伝[[神功皇后]]陵）等とともに西群を形成する{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。佐紀高塚古墳自体は、佐紀石塚山古墳・佐紀陵山古墳の南に隣接する形で築造される{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[明治]]以降は[[宮内庁]]により[[天皇陵]]に治定されているため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けており、南北を主軸とする西群の他古墳とは直交する{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘からはかつて[[埴輪]]が検出されたというが詳らかでない{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。墳丘周囲には不連続ながら鍵穴形で幅の狭い周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 この佐紀高塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期後半の[[4世紀]]代の築造と推定される{{Sfn|高野陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。佐紀古墳群の古墳としては中型規模で、墳丘主軸も他の主要古墳とは方向を異にすることから、主要古墳に随伴するランクの古墳であったものと推測される{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、現在では宮内庁により第48代[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]（第46代孝謙天皇重祚）の陵に治定されている{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}。 なお古墳域は、後世に営まれた[[平城京]]では右京北辺二坊に位置する{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|p=19}}。また近年には古墳西方を流れる秋篠川において河川改修時に多数の大型[[円筒埴輪]]が出土しているが、それらを本古墳のものとする説もある{{Sfn|高野陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）、文久の修陵で称徳天皇の陵に治定{{Sfn|奈良市史 考古編|1971|pp=125-126}}。 * [[明治]]期、宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により称徳天皇の陵に治定。 * [[1889年]]（明治22年）、石柵・鉄扉の改造{{Sfn|奈良市史 考古編|1971|pp=125-126}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：127メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：84メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：70メートル == 被葬者 == 佐紀高塚古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第48代'''[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]'''（第46代'''孝謙天皇'''重祚）の陵に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/14}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）14コマ。{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 409。。『[[続日本紀]]』によれば、称徳天皇は[[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）8月4日に西宮寝殿で崩御したため、即日に作山陵司等の諸司が任じられ、[[鈴鹿王]]（[[高市皇子]]次男）の旧宅の場所に畿内・近国の役夫6,300人をして山陵が営造され、8月17日に天皇をその[[大和国]][[添下郡]]佐貴郷の「高野山陵」に葬ったという{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|高野陵（平凡社）|1981}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠陵の「高野陵」として記載され、大和国添下郡の所在で、兆域は東西5町・南北3町で守戸5烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}。 称徳天皇の真陵については、『西大寺資財流記帳』の記述を基にすると、この佐紀高塚古墳（[[西大寺 (奈良市)|西大寺]]の東方に所在）ではなく西大寺の寺域西限の地に存在したと推定される『続日本紀 3（東洋文庫524）』 平凡社、1990年、p. 299。{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|p=13}}。しかし後世に陵に関する所伝は失われ、[[鎌倉時代]]の「大和国西大寺往古敷地図」（[[徳治]]2年（[[1307年]]）以前成立）を初見として本古墳に比定する説が生じた{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。[[江戸時代]]には、さらに北方の[[五社神古墳]]を称徳天皇陵にあてた説も生じている{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。その後、幕末の文久の修陵に際し、「大和国西大寺往古敷地図」等を基に本古墳が改めて称徳天皇陵に比定され、これが現在に踏襲されている{{Sfn|高野陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。 なお、本来の称徳天皇陵の位置については明らかでないが、西大寺西方にある奈良自動車学校の南側丘陵付近に推定する説がある{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|pp=36-39}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]山陵町字御陵前 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道 ** [[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[近鉄京都線|京都線]] [[平城駅]]（徒歩約6分） ** 近畿日本鉄道（近鉄）京都線・[[近鉄奈良線|奈良線]]・[[近鉄橿原線|橿原線]] [[大和西大寺駅]]（徒歩約7分） '''周辺''' * 成務天皇 狭城盾列池後陵（[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]） * 日葉酢媛命 狭木之寺間陵（[[佐紀陵山古墳]]） * 神功皇后 狭城盾列池上陵（[[五社神古墳]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1971|chapter=|title=奈良市史 考古編|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|pages=125-126|ref={{Harvid|奈良市史 考古編|1971}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=林陸朗 「孝謙天皇」|ref={{Harvid|孝謙天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=戸原純一 「高野陵」（孝謙天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|高野陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=高野陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|高野陵（平凡社）|1981}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|高野陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本裕行|year=1989|chapter=称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|称徳（孝謙）天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|author=西崎卓哉|year=2001|chapter=佐紀高塚古墳|title=大和前方後円墳集成|publisher=[[学生社]]|isbn=4311303270|ref={{Harvid|佐紀高塚古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=今尾文昭|authorlink=今尾文昭|year=2014|chapter=|title=ヤマト政権の一大勢力 佐紀古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」093）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787713339|ref={{Harvid|今尾文昭|2014}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[孝謙天皇]] == 外部リンク == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Shōtoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E4%BD%90%E7%B4%80%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:aki Takatsuka Kofun]]

Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nara Line|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kashihara Line|qq=}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimu|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū|qq=}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nara Line|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kashihara Line|qq=}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimu|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū|qq=}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Line|ja|近鉄奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nara Line|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kashihara Line|qq=}}: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimu|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū|qq=}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi
