This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} == See Also == * (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[:en:Yamato-Saidaiji Station|Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' Kofun]][[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E4%BD%90%E7%B4%80%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:aki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] (Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[:en:Yamato-Saidaiji Station|Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' Kofun]][[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:E4%BD%90%E7%B4%80%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:aki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] Mound Overview
(Front section in foreground on the right, rear circular section in background on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前 | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[:en:Yamato-Saidaiji Station|Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' Kofun]][[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:E4%BD%90%E7%B4%80%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:aki Takatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}''' (earlier reigned
