[[sn:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27898021]] Wikidata name: Saki Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[:en:Yamato-Saidaiji Station|Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q27898021}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27898021]] Wikidata name: Saki Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[:en:Yamato-Saidaiji Station|Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q27898021}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27898021]] Wikidata name: Saki Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[:en:Yamato-Saidaiji Station|Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q27898021}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27898021]] Wikidata name: Saki Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | built = [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | Imperial tomb = [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Shōtoku]] (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]] Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] '''Access''' * Railway ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]], about 6 minutes on foot ** [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]], [[:en:Nara Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nara Line]], and [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]]: [[:en:Yamato-Saidaiji Station|Yamato-Saidaiji Station]], about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]]'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q27898021}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Empress Kōken]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27898021]] Wikidata name: Saki Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line}}: {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station}}, about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line}}, {{ill|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q692386|lt=Kintetsu Nara Line}}, and {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}: {{ill|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|en|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|WD=Q489786|lt=Yamato-Saidaiji Station}}, about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. [[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27898021]] Wikidata name: Saki Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line}}: {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station}}, about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line}}, {{ill|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q692386|lt=Kintetsu Nara Line}}, and {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}: {{ill|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|en|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|WD=Q489786|lt=Yamato-Saidaiji Station}}, about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku}} (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line}}: {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station}}, about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line}}, {{ill|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q692386|lt=Kintetsu Nara Line}}, and {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}: {{ill|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|en|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|WD=Q489786|lt=Yamato-Saidaiji Station}}, about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]]
