revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846|1=Misasagi-chō}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara|1=Nara (city)}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}: {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station|1=Heijō Station}}, about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}, {{ill|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q692386|lt=Kintetsu Nara Line|1=Nara Line (Kintetsu)}}, and {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}}: {{ill|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|en|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|WD=Q489786|lt=Yamato-Saidaiji Station|1=Yamato-Saidaiji Station}}, about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Saki Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]][[zh:佐紀高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Saki Takatsuka Kofun | image = File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846|1=Misasagi-chō}} | coordinates deg = 34| coordinates min = 41| coordinates sec = 54.00 | coordinates deg = 135| coordinates min = 47| coordinates sec = 15.05 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} | burial = Unknown | area = Mound length 127m | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1) 48th Emperor {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} | excavations = (Reportedly) {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | Imperial tomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation1 "Takano no Misasagi" | notes = | map = Nara city | type = 前方後円墳-西南西 | map_dot_label = Saki Takatsuka
Kofun | coordinates = {{Coord|34.698336|N|135.787517|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Kōken|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}} }} [[File:Saki Takatsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}} at Takano no Misasagi}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Takatsuka Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree view of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at Takano no Misasagi}} '''Saki Takatsuka Kofun''' (佐紀高塚古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (Western Group). The actual interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the "'''Takano no Misasagi'''" (高野陵), the mausoleum of the 48th {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=Empress Kōken}} (who also reigned earlier as the 46th Empress, Kōken). == Overview == [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{center|{{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} Western Group}}]] The Saki Takatsuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound located on the southern slopes of the Saki Hills at the northern edge of the Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting into the edge of the hill{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Hills are home to many large-map_size kofun within the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. This mound belongs to the Western Group, along with the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}), the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (purported tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}){{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The Saki Takatsuka Kofun was built adjacent to the southern sides of the Saki Ishizuka Kofun and the Saki Ryozan Kofun{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. The mound's type is keyhole-like, with the front section oriented westward, perpendicular to the north-south axis of the other mounds in the Western Group{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The mound was built in three tiers{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. It is reported to have originally featured haniwa (clay figures), but details remain unclear{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. A narrow, discontinuous moat encircles the mound in a roughly keyhole-like type{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}{{Sfn|Imao Bunshō|2014|pp=36-39}}. The burial facility and grave goods remain unknown, as the site has not been subjected to detailed investigation due to its Historic Site as an imperial tomb{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano Mausoleum (National History)}}. == location == '''location''' * {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara|1=Nara (city)}} Yamamichi-chō字御陵前, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}: {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station|1=Heijō Station}}, about 6 minutes on foot ** {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}, {{ill|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nara Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q692386|lt=Kintetsu Nara Line|1=Nara Line (Kintetsu)}}, and {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}}: {{ill|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|en|Yamato-Saidaiji Station|WD=Q489786|lt=Yamato-Saidaiji Station|1=Yamato-Saidaiji Station}}, about 7 minutes on foot '''Nearby Sites''' * Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}'s {{ill|Saki Ishizuka Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}'s {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Empress {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == See Also == * '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|version=99995019|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Saki Takatsuka Kofun}} [[ja:佐紀高塚古墳]] Kofun|1989}}. == area == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shōtoku (Kōken) Mausoleum Kofun|1989}}: * Total length: 127 meters * Rear circular section: 84 meters in diameter, three tiers * Front rectangular section: 70 meters in width, three tiers == burial == The interred individual of the Saki Takatsuka Kofun is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the tomb of the 48th Emperor, '''{{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}''' (earlier reigned as the 46th Emperor, '''Kōken''')[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Mausoleums] (Imperial Household Agency).Imperial Household Agency, {{NDLDC|1237123/14}} ''Ryo-bo Yōran'' (1934).{{Sfn|Takano
